$notes.ntz_schema_requires_feature: The spec requires timestampNtz to be listed in both readerFeatures and writerFeatures when the schema contains timestamp_ntz columns. A timestamp_ntz schema without the feature listed is an explicitly invalid table state; failure is spec-grounded rather than errata.

cases.name: timestamp_ntz_create_table_3_7_feature_listed_succeeds
cases.description: Creating a (3,7)+[timestampNtz] table with a timestamp_ntz column succeeds.

cases.name: timestamp_ntz_create_table_1_2_with_ntz_schema_fails
cases.description: Creating a (1,2) table with a timestamp_ntz column fails; timestampNtz requires minReaderVersion=3, minWriterVersion=7, and the feature in both feature lists.

cases.name: timestamp_ntz_create_table_3_7_no_feature_listed_with_schema_fails
cases.description: Creating a (3,7) table with a timestamp_ntz column but timestampNtz absent from both feature lists fails.

cases.name: timestamp_ntz_read_snapshot_3_7_feature_listed_succeeds
cases.description: Reading a (3,7)+[timestampNtz] table with a timestamp_ntz column succeeds.

cases.name: timestamp_ntz_read_snapshot_3_7_nested_in_array_succeeds
cases.description: Reading a (3,7)+[timestampNtz] table with a timestamp_ntz array element column succeeds; the feature requirement applies regardless of nesting depth.

cases.name: timestamp_ntz_supported_not_active_read_snapshot_succeeds
cases.description: Reading a (3,7)+[timestampNtz] table with no timestamp_ntz columns in the schema succeeds.
cases.note: timestampNtz is active only when the schema contains at least one timestamp_ntz column.

cases.name: timestamp_ntz_read_snapshot_3_7_no_feature_listed_with_schema_fails
cases.description: Reading a (3,7) table with a timestamp_ntz column but timestampNtz absent from both feature lists fails.

cases.name: timestamp_ntz_read_snapshot_1_2_with_ntz_schema_fails
cases.description: Reading a (1,2) table with a timestamp_ntz column fails; the protocol is insufficient to support timestampNtz.

cases.name: timestamp_ntz_empty_commit_3_7_feature_listed_succeeds
cases.description: Appending to a (3,7)+[timestampNtz] table with a timestamp_ntz column succeeds.

cases.name: timestamp_ntz_empty_commit_supported_not_active_succeeds
cases.description: Appending to a (3,7)+[timestampNtz] table with no timestamp_ntz columns in the schema succeeds.

cases.name: timestamp_ntz_empty_commit_3_7_no_feature_listed_with_schema_fails
cases.description: Appending to a (3,7) table with a timestamp_ntz column but timestampNtz absent from both feature lists fails.

cases.name: timestamp_ntz_empty_commit_1_2_with_ntz_schema_fails
cases.description: Appending to a (1,2) table with a timestamp_ntz column fails; the protocol is insufficient to support timestampNtz.

cases.name: timestamp_ntz_type_widening_date_to_ntz_both_features_read_snapshot_succeeds
cases.description: Reading a (3,7)+[timestampNtz, typeWidening] table where a column was widened from date to timestamp_ntz succeeds.

cases.name: timestamp_ntz_type_widening_date_to_ntz_missing_ntz_feature_read_snapshot_fails
cases.description: Reading a (3,7)+[typeWidening] table where a column was widened from date to timestamp_ntz but timestampNtz is absent from both feature lists fails; the resulting timestamp_ntz column independently requires the timestampNtz feature.
